content:
10/20

Hi guys, I'm facing a strange build error while trying to compile the rust-next branch which is downloaded from the following link:

https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux.git

Here are the details of my environment:

Ubuntu 25.10 (running as a VM on top of kubuntu 24.04 LTS).
The vm is KVM based.

my dot config: https://pastebin.com/UXxyfbRs

Note: I have used the vm's dot-config file from /boot/config-xxxx-yyy as .config

I've followed the Quick start guide : https://docs.kernel.org/rust/quick-start.html

And used following commands in sequence

  1. apt install rustc rust-src bindgen rustfmt rust-clippy
  2. export RUST_LIB_SRC=/usr/src/rustc-$(rustc --version | cut -d' ' -f2)/library
  3. make LLVM=1 rustavailable
  4. make LLVM=1 -j6

Error that I got is as follows:

CC [M] drivers/input/tablet/wacom_serial4.o
drivers/net/ethernet/mellanox/mlx5/core/en/params.o: error: objtool: mlx5e_mpwrq_mtts_per_wqe() falls through to next function mlx5e_mpwrq_max_num_entries()
make[7]: *** [scripts/Makefile.build:287: drivers/net/ethernet/mellanox/mlx5/core/en/params.o] Error 1
make[7]: *** Deleting file 'drivers/net/ethernet/mellanox/mlx5/core/en/params.o'
make[6]: *** [scripts/Makefile.build:556: drivers/net/ethernet/mellanox/mlx5/core] Error 2
make[5]: *** [scripts/Makefile.build:556: drivers/net/ethernet/mellanox] Error 2
make[5]: *** Waiting for unfinished jobs....

Now, I have searched and found that following patch fixes this bug but even after that it breaks something else

link to the patch: https://www.spinics.net/lists/netdev/msg1129039.html

Post patch the second compilation error is as follows:

CC [M] drivers/watchdog/ib700wdt.o
drivers/media/i2c/ccs/ccs-core.o: error: objtool: ccs_set_selection() falls through to next function ccs_propagate()
make[6]: *** [scripts/Makefile.build:287: drivers/media/i2c/ccs/ccs-core.o] Error 1
make[6]: *** Deleting file 'drivers/media/i2c/ccs/ccs-core.o'
make[5]: *** [scripts/Makefile.build:556: drivers/media/i2c/ccs] Error 2
make[4]: *** [scripts/Makefile.build:556: drivers/media/i2c] Error 2
make[3]: *** [scripts/Makefile.build:556: drivers/media] Error 2
make[3]: *** Waiting for unfinished jobs....
CC [M] drivers/hwmon/ltc4151.o

I understand that distro dot-configs are typically "all-inclusive" and the error is not related to rust in particular but I checked online and could not get any pointers. I would really appreciate if I get help on this pressing issue or a working dot-config that would just work as I would be installing the newly compiled kernel and test it out.

Thank you.

Yeah, it is unrelated to Rust. If you can, please report it to the LKML. Meanwhile, if you want to ignore those errors, you could disable OBJTOOL_WERROR in the configuration, but it is not recommended. Another option is to disable those modules if you don't need them.
From a quick look, I see that those two at least are already known: https://lore.kernel.org/all/20250131204920.GA1974131@ax162/

Thank you Miguel for your quick reply. I have found a workaround. Instead of copying the dot-config from /boot I have executed

make LLVM=1 localmodconfig

That did the trick.

AS arch/x86/boot/header.o
LD arch/x86/boot/setup.elf
OBJCOPY arch/x86/boot/setup.bin
BUILD arch/x86/boot/bzImage
kernel: arch/x86/boot/bzImage is ready (#1)

real 13m53.387s
user 63m23.288s
sys 5m16.746s

Now I can proceed with the next steps. This issue can be marked as "solved".

Thank you.
localmodconfig likely disabled those modules (and many others), i.e. it is an instance of the alternative I was mentioning.
You're welcome! I think you should be able to resolve it yourself using the tick above, but if that doesn't work, we can do it for you.
I cannot resolve it as it says that I don't have the required permission.
Marius Bock has marked this topic as resolved.
